message.content:
Adversarial refuter: ASSUME this finding is FALSE; disprove it from the code at /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay. survives=true only if you genuinely cannot.
P7-SUPPLY-CHAIN-2: License gate scans entire site-packages (`--with-system`), not the hash-pinned set the comment claims
/Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/.github/workflows/ci.yml:192
The license-gate step comment (lines 184-189) asserts it scans 'the SAME hash-pinned set pip-audit audited, not a live resolution.' In practice the command is `pip-licenses --format=json --with-system` (line 193), which enumerates EVERY distribution in site-packages. By that point the env also contains `pip-licenses==5.5.5` and its transitive deps (prettytable, wcwidth, etc.) installed at line 192, plus pip/setuptools/wheel via `--with-system`, plus the live-fetched build backend. So the license-checked set is a superset of `requirements.lock`, contradicting the comment and coupling the production-distribution license verdict to dev/tooling packages. A copyleft license appearing in a pip-licenses transitive dep (or any tool dep) would spuriously fail the gate, or conversely could be (mis)read as part of the distributed set. Additionally, `pip-licenses==5.5.5` is version-pinned but installed without `--require-hashes` (line 192), unlike every other install in this job — a minor pinning inconsistency.
PoC: `pip-licenses --with-system` output length != number of packages in requirements.lock; it includes pip-licenses, prettytable, wcwidth, pip, setuptools, wheel and the build backend, none of which are in requirements.lock.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.content:
180	          name: sbom-cyclonedx
181	          path: sbom.cyclonedx.json
182	          if-no-files-found: error
183	      - name: License gate (deny strong-copyleft in the pinned dependency set)
184	        run: |
185	          set -euo pipefail
186	          # Fail if any dependency carries a strong-copyleft license incompatible with the
187	          # Apache-2.0 distribution (#147). Scan the SAME hash-pinned set pip-audit audited, not a
188	          # live `pip install -e .[all]` resolution that could pull different versions than the
189	          # lockfile pins (#F-013). Advisory monitoring of the rest is handled by Dependabot.
190	          pip install --require-hashes -r requirements.lock
191	          pip install --no-deps -e .
192	          python -m pip install pip-licenses==5.5.5  # pinned for reproducible license output (#G-022)
193	          pip-licenses --format=json --with-system > licenses.json
194	          python scripts/license_gate.py licenses.json
195	      - name: Upload license report
196	        if: always()
197	        uses: actions/upload-artifact@ea165f8d65b6e75b540449e92b4886f43607fa02 # v4
198	        with:
199	          name: license-report
200	          path: licenses.json
201	          if-no-files-found: warn
202	
203	  # Single required status check (#F-014): branch protection should require ONLY `all-checks`.
204	  # It fans in every other job, so adding a new job automatically gates merges without anyone
205	  # remembering to update the branch-protection rule — and `audit`/`integration` can never be
206	  # silently dropped from the merge gate by requiring just `core`.
207	  all-checks:
208	    if: always()
209	    needs: [core, integration, audit]

content.text: The finding's core claim is confirmed at the source level. Now let me check the `license_gate.py` script to see if it filters the package set, which could disprove the practical impact.

content.content:
1	"""License gate (#147): fail if any installed dependency carries a strong-copyleft license.
2	
3	Reads a pip-licenses JSON report (path as argv[1], default ``licenses.json``) and exits non-zero
4	if any package's license matches a denied strong-copyleft family that would be incompatible with
5	the Apache-2.0 distribution. LGPL (weak copyleft, dynamic linking) is permitted. Advisory
6	monitoring of the rest is handled by Dependabot (.github/dependabot.yml).
7	"""
8	
9	from __future__ import annotations
10	
11	import json
12	import sys
13	
14	# Strong-copyleft families denied in the distributed dependency set. Both the SPDX-style short
15	# tokens AND the full prose names must be listed (pass 3, #F-015): a package reporting
16	# "Server Side Public License" or "European Union Public Licence 1.2" contains neither the "SSPL"
17	# nor "EUPL" token, so the short tokens alone let those families bypass the gate.
18	_DENY = (
19	    "GPL",
20	    "AGPL",
21	    "SSPL",
22	    "EUPL",
23	    "SERVER SIDE PUBLIC",  # SSPL prose form
24	    "EUROPEAN UNION PUBLIC",  # EUPL prose form
25	    "AFFERO",  # AGPL prose form ("GNU Affero General Public License")
26	)
27	
28	
29	def main(path: str = "licenses.json") -> int:
30	    with open(path, encoding="utf-8") as f:  # context manager — no leaked fd (#F-050)
31	        packages = json.load(f)
32	    bad = []
33	    unknown = []
34	    for pkg in packages:
35	        raw = (pkg.get("License") or "").strip()
36	        lic = raw.upper()
37	        # A package whose license is empty / UNKNOWN / unspecified could be hiding a copyleft the
38	        # deny-substring scan can't see. Don't fail on it (pip-licenses reports UNKNOWN for many
39	        # legitimate packages with incomplete metadata), but SURFACE it as a CI warning so a
40	        # reviewer checks it instead of it passing silently (#H-007).
41	        if not raw or lic in ("UNKNOWN", "UNKNOWN LICENSE", "NONE"):
42	            unknown.append(f"{pkg.get('Name')} {pkg.get('Version')}")
43	            continue
44	        if "LGPL" in lic:  # weak copyleft — permitted for dynamically-linked libraries
45	            continue
46	        if any(tok in lic for tok in _DENY):
47	            bad.append(f"{pkg.get('Name')} {pkg.get('Version')}: {pkg.get('License')}")
48	    if unknown:
49	        print(
50	            "::warning::license gate: "
51	            + str(len(unknown))
52	            + " package(s) report no/UNKNOWN license — review for hidden copyleft: "
53	            + ", ".join(sorted(unknown))
54	        )
55	    if bad:
56	        print("::error::strong-copyleft licenses found:\n" + "\n".join(bad))
57	        return 1
58	    print(f"license gate OK ({len(packages)} packages checked, {len(unknown)} unknown)")
59	    return 0
60	
61	
62	if __name__ == "__main__":
63	    raise SystemExit(main(sys.argv[1] if len(sys.argv) > 1 else "licenses.json"))
64

content.text:
The script does no filtering — it iterates over every package in the JSON, which is the full `--with-system` set. This confirms the finding's central claim.

Let me verify the secondary claims: that `pip-licenses==5.5.5` is installed without `--require-hashes` (already visible at line 192), and confirm the comment's wording.

The evidence is conclusive:

1. **Comment claim (lines 187-189):** "Scan the SAME hash-pinned set pip-audit audited, not a live `pip install -e .[all]` resolution."
2. **Actual command (line 193):** `pip-licenses --format=json --with-system` — `--with-system` explicitly includes pip, setuptools, wheel, and all system/tooling packages.
3. **No filtering downstream:** `license_gate.py` iterates `for pkg in packages` over the entire JSON with zero restriction to `requirements.lock` members. The final message even prints `len(packages)` as the count "checked."
4. **Env contamination:** By line 193, the env contains `pip-licenses==5.5.5` plus its transitive deps (prettytable, wcwidth, etc.) installed at line 192, the `-e .` editable install at 191, and `--with-system` packages.
5. **Pinning inconsistency:** Line 192 `python -m pip install pip-licenses==5.5.5` lacks `--require-hashes`, unlike lines 190 and the rest of the job.

The set scanned is unambiguously a superset of `requirements.lock`, directly contradicting the comment.

I attempted to disprove this finding but could not. The only path to disproving it would have been if `license_gate.py` filtered the package set back down to the lockfile members — it does not; it checks every package in the `--with-system` output. The finding is accurate.

input.reasoning: Confirmed accurate. .github/workflows/ci.yml:193 runs `pip-licenses --format=json --with-system`, and `--with-system` enumerates EVERY distribution in site-packages — including pip/setuptools/wheel, the editable `-e .` install (line 191), and pip-licenses==5.5.5 plus its transitive deps (prettytable, wcwidth, etc.) installed at line 192. The downstream consumer scripts/license_gate.py does NO filtering: it iterates `for pkg in packages` over the entire JSON and even reports `len(packages)` as the count "checked." So the license-checked set is a strict superset of requirements.lock, directly contradicting the comment at lines 187-189 ("Scan the SAME hash-pinned set pip-audit audited"). A copyleft license in any tooling/transitive dep would spuriously fail (or be misattributed to the distributed set). The secondary claim also holds: line 192 installs pip-licenses==5.5.5 WITHOUT `--require-hashes`, unlike line 190 and the rest of the job — a pinning inconsistency. I could not disprove any part of the finding.

content.text: Finding P7-SUPPLY-CHAIN-2 survives. The license gate at `/Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/.github/workflows/ci.yml:193` does scan the full `--with-system` package set (a superset of `requirements.lock`), and `/Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/scripts/license_gate.py` performs no filtering to reduce it back to the pinned set — directly contradicting the comment at lines 187-189. The secondary pinning inconsistency (line 192 lacks `--require-hashes`) also holds.
